layouts.name: PhoneColumnScrollShell
layouts.importPath: (pattern only — no bundled reference page; see layout-candidates.md)
layouts.description: Product phone frame: fixed-width column (--phone-column-width), full viewport height, flex column, inner scroll region (.sh-content), optional sticky bottom chrome (.sh-bottom-nav), section rhythm (.sh-section / .sh-section__content).
layouts.purpose: Standard in-app screen structure for PML: one mobile-width column with tokenized surfaces, scrollable main stack, and optional BottomNav that stays pinned to the bottom of the column.

props.pattern: Implemented with CSS classes, not a standalone layout package — mirror the documented `.stock-home` / `.sh-content` geometry (or same flex + scroll rules with a page-specific BEM prefix).
props.colorScheme: light | dark — optional page prop for Header status bar and BrandLogo alignment; does not change flex geometry.
props.scrollRegionContent: ReactNode — everything that belongs inside the `flex: 1; overflow-y: auto` region (e.g. Header + body on a typical home screen; other pages may differ).
props.bottomChrome: ReactNode | omit — typically `BottomNav` wrapped in `.sh-bottom-nav` (or equivalent); omit for flows without tab bar (e.g. login).

allowedChildren: Inside the scroll region: `Header`, widgets, `SectionHeader`, cards, lists — composed with section wrappers (e.g. `.sh-section` + `.sh-section__content` with `margin-top: var(--spacing-56)` on sections).
Optional sibling below scroll: bottom chrome (e.g. `BottomNav` in `.sh-bottom-nav`) — `flex-shrink: 0`, not inside the overflow-y region.
New screens may use a page-specific root class if they preserve the same flex + scroll + token rules as the documented phone-column shell.
Do not place multiple independent `100vh` columns inside one shell; one product column per screen root.
layoutRules: Root: `width: var(--phone-column-width); height: 100vh; display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: var(--surface-level-4); overflow: hidden; text-align: left;` (canonical class `.stock-home` in historical Stock Home CSS).

Scroll region: child with `flex: 1; min-height: 0; overflow-y: auto; overflow-x: hidden; padding-bottom: var(--spacing-16)` (`.sh-content`). Only this region scrolls vertically; the root stays `overflow: hidden`.

On a typical home layout, `Header` lives inside `.sh-content` and scrolls with content; `width: 100%; flex-shrink: 0` on the header row class. If a screen needs a fixed header above scroll (e.g. bill pay), document that deviation explicitly.
Bottom nav: sibling of `.sh-content` under the root, wrapped in `.sh-bottom-nav` — `flex-shrink: 0`, optional `z-index` for stacking; must not be inside the scrolling div.

Sections: use `.sh-section` with `margin-top: var(--spacing-56)` (first section may use design-approved exceptions); content wells use `.sh-section__content` with `padding: var(--spacing-16)` unless a widget owns its own horizontal inset.
Use design tokens only for shell chrome (`--spacing-*`, `--surface-*`, `--font-family`); do not hardcode 376px when `--phone-column-width` applies.
Horizontal carousels: follow established overflow-x patterns (e.g. `.sh-hscroll` / widget CSS); do not break the column width or add gutters that conflict with `--spacing-16` rhythm.
responsiveRules: The canonical shell CSS defines no media queries: the column stays `var(--phone-column-width)`; the shell does not reflow to tablet or desktop grids.
Wider viewports show the column as-is, centered by the host layout (e.g. Storybook canvas or a custom wrapper) — not by this pattern alone.
No `env(safe-area-inset-*)` in the canonical CSS; status bar is handled via `Header` props, not this layout file alone.
notes: Pattern-based today: there is no `layout/PhoneColumnScrollShell.tsx` export. Bundled Stock Home / Discover reference pages were removed — use `layout-candidates.md`, `docs/LEARNINGS.md`, and Storybook compositions when rebuilding.
`importPath` is intentionally non-module: implement by copying the documented class structure into new pages until a shared shell component (e.g. `PhoneScrollShell`) is extracted.
Login-style screens use the column + scroll stack without `BottomNav`; some bill-pay style screens use header outside the scroll region — valid variants, not the default header-in-scroll model.
